title: Deduplicate EPIC_ID_PATTERN — import from cli-utils
description: phase-check.ts has a private EPIC_ID_PATTERN identical to the exported one in cli-utils.ts. Remove the local copy and import from cli-utils.ts. Both reviewers flagged Minor.

title: Capture lesson about worktree state routing
description: After implementation is complete, capture a compound-agent lesson via npx ca learn about the worktree state routing constraint. Key insight: Claude Code hooks always run with cwd=primary working directory. When a git worktree is added as an additional directory (not primary), all .ca-* state files (.ca-phase-state.json, .ca-failure-state.json) are misrouted to the main repo. The fix is architectural: worktrees MUST be opened as the primary working directory in a separate Claude Code session. This is an inherent Claude Code limitation (hooks are dispatched from primary dir, settings.json is only read from primary dir). Tags: worktree, hooks, architecture, state-routing.

title: Fix knowledge embedding system production readiness
description: Fix 20+ issues from code reviews: lint errors, inMemory bug, connection version logic, hybrid search filtering, schema cleanup, dead code removal, P2/P3 fixes
close_reason: Fixed all 20+ issues: lint errors, inMemory bug, connection version logic, hybrid search filtering, schema v2, dead code removal, P2/P3 fixes

title: Update set-worktree SKILL.md with primary-dir requirement
description: File: .claude/skills/compound/set-worktree/SKILL.md. Update the skill documentation to make the 'worktree must be primary directory' requirement explicit. Add to Methodology section after step 7: '8. Instruct the user to open the worktree as the PRIMARY working directory in a NEW Claude Code session (cd <path> && claude). Warn them NOT to add it as an additional directory to an existing session.' Add to Common Pitfalls: 'Opening the worktree as an additional directory instead of primary (hooks and phase state will be misrouted to the main repo)'. Add a new section explaining WHY: Claude Code hooks always run with cwd=primary dir, settings.json is only read from primary dir, so all state files (.ca-phase-state.json, .ca-failure-state.json) would go to the wrong location.' Acceptance: (1) Methodology updated, (2) Common Pitfalls updated, (3) Why section added.

title: Fix validateEpicId mock in worktree.test.ts
description: The mock allows dots in epic IDs but the real validateEpicId does not. Fix mock to match real pattern. Sonnet flagged Major test quality gap.

title: Fix knowledge embedding system production readiness (23 issues)
description: Deep review revealed 23 issues P0-P3. Critical: all production calls hardcode inMemory:true making system non-functional. Fix all issues in single pass.

title: Guard getRepoRoot() against empty-string env var
description: Change getRepoRoot() from ?? to || so empty string COMPOUND_AGENT_ROOT falls back to process.cwd(). Sonnet flagged Minor.

title: Add 72h TTL to .ca-phase-state.json reads
description: File: src/commands/phase-check.ts, function getPhaseState() (lines 103-113). After parsing and validating the state, add a staleness check: if Date.now() - Date.parse(state.started_at) > 72 * 60 * 60 * 1000, call cleanPhaseState(repoRoot) and return null. This prevents stale phase state from interrupted LFG sessions persisting indefinitely. The 72h window (vs 24h) covers multi-day epics that pause overnight. Pattern already exists in hooks.ts readFailureState() (1h TTL at line 183). Acceptance: (1) TTL check added, (2) stale state auto-cleaned, (3) test for TTL expiry, (4) test that 71h state is still valid.

title: Replace process.cwd() with getRepoRoot() in phase-check.ts
description: File: src/commands/phase-check.ts, line 267. Currently: const repoRoot = (): string => process.cwd(); Must change to: const repoRoot = (): string => getRepoRoot(); Import getRepoRoot from ../cli-utils.js. This makes phase-check CLI commands (init, start, gate, status, clean) respect the COMPOUND_AGENT_ROOT env var. Zero regression risk since getRepoRoot() falls back to process.cwd() when env var is unset. Acceptance: (1) import added, (2) line 267 updated, (3) existing tests still pass, (4) new test verifying COMPOUND_AGENT_ROOT is respected.

title: Clean up stale phase state file on TTL expiry
description: In getPhaseState(), when TTL check fails (age > 72h), call cleanPhaseState(repoRoot) before returning null. Currently stale files accumulate on disk forever. Both reviewers flagged Major.

title: Review fixes: post-worktree-isolation cleanup
description: Address findings from Opus and Sonnet review of commit 1024098 (worktree state file isolation). 5 items: behavioral hook test, TTL cleanup, dedup EPIC_ID_PATTERN, empty-string guard, fix validateEpicId mock.

title: Improve ca worktree create output with next-step command
description:
File: src/commands/worktree.ts, function addCreateCommand() around lines 358-362. After the current output (Path, Branch, Merge task), add a clear next-step message: 'Next step: Start a new Claude Code session in the worktree:
  cd <worktreePath> && claude

Then run: /compound:lfg "your goal"

IMPORTANT: The worktree must be the PRIMARY working directory.
Do NOT add it as an additional directory to an existing session.
Hooks and phase state will not work correctly otherwise.' This is critical UX because Claude Code hooks always run with cwd=primary dir. If the worktree is only an additional dir, all state files go to the main repo instead. Acceptance: (1) output includes the cd && claude command, (2) output includes the warning about primary dir, (3) test updated to verify new output.

title: Epic: Fix .ca-* state files in worktree context
description: Phase state files (.ca-phase-state.json, .ca-failure-state.json) are misrouted when worktrees are used as additional directories instead of primary working directories. All hooks and phase-check CLI resolve paths via bare process.cwd(), which points to the main repo when the worktree is an additional dir. This causes: (1) state pollution in the main repo, (2) stale files on interrupted LFG sessions, (3) cross-epic conflicts with multiple worktrees. Root cause: Claude Code hooks always run with cwd=primary dir. Hooks from additional dirs' settings.json are never loaded. Fix approach: (a) Unify path resolution via getRepoRoot() for COMPOUND_AGENT_ROOT support, (b) Add 72h TTL to phase state, (c) Improve ca worktree create UX with clear next-step command, (d) Update documentation. Confirmed by independent Opus and Sonnet architectural reviews. Blocks v1.3.7 release.

title: Add behavioral hook test with COMPOUND_AGENT_ROOT
description: Replace or supplement hooks-repo-root.test.ts structural test with a behavioral test: set COMPOUND_AGENT_ROOT env var, invoke hook handler, verify state file lands in COMPOUND_AGENT_ROOT/.claude/ not process.cwd()/.claude/. Both Opus and Sonnet flagged this as Major.

title: Replace process.cwd() with getRepoRoot() in hooks.ts
description: File: src/setup/hooks.ts. Four occurrences to fix: (1) line 500 in runPostToolFailureHook: const stateDir = join(process.cwd(), '.claude') -> join(getRepoRoot(), '.claude'), (2) line 515 in runPostToolSuccessHook: same change, (3) line 531 in runToolHook: processor(process.cwd(), ...) -> processor(getRepoRoot(), ...), (4) line 540 in runStopAuditHook: processStopAudit(process.cwd(), ...) -> processStopAudit(getRepoRoot(), ...). Import getRepoRoot from ../cli-utils.js. Acceptance: (1) all 4 occurrences updated, (2) import added, (3) existing tests pass, (4) new test verifying COMPOUND_AGENT_ROOT propagates through hook runners.
